name: @platform/queue-runtime
description: Queue abstraction: job enqueue interface, queue topology contracts, and delivery guarantee definitions.

readme.summary: Queue abstraction: job enqueue interface, queue topology contracts, and delivery guarantee definitions.
responsibilities: Define the typed job enqueue interface and job payload schemas
Declare queue topology contracts (queue names, job types, priority levels)
Specify delivery guarantee semantics (at-least-once, exactly-once) per queue
Decouple producers and consumers from the specific queue broker
nonResponsibilities: Does not implement the queue broker connection (owned by adapters-redis/BullMQ)
Does not own worker job processors (owned by worker-runtime and feature packages)
Does not manage dead-letter queue reprocessing
usage: Imported by feature packages to enqueue jobs without coupling to the broker
worker-runtime imports topology contracts to register consumers
adapters-redis implements the enqueue interface against BullMQ
operationalNotes: Queue name changes require coordinated migration ? names are part of the topology contract
Exactly-once delivery is not guaranteed by default ? idempotent job processors are required
